meaning: adj.爱德华七世时代的n.爱德华七世时代的人
examples: Such practice, it seems, was comme il faut for the idle rich in Edwardian New York.
But then the Games went off as peacefully as an Edwardian field day.
What am I doing swanning around Europe as if I am some young Edwardian doing the Grand Tour?
It would have been grand to traipse down to the Edwardian Room for dinner.
This description could equally fit Edwardian Britain on the eve of World War I and America today.
